title: Dto Graph Impact
description: Client-side type for **Dto Graph Impact**, kept in parity with the matching `api:dto-*` spec so remote and embedded Studio render the same JSON the API emits.
context: Client-side type for **Dto Graph Impact**, kept in parity with the matching `api:dto-*` spec so remote and embedded Studio render the same JSON the API emits.

rules.requirement: client DTO matches API wire shape
rules: Field names, optional/required semantics, and nesting MUST match the paired `api:dto-*` spec. The client MUST NOT invent alternate property names.

rules.requirement: types are shared or generated from API schemas
rules: DTO types MUST be imported from a shared package or generated from the same schema source used by API presenters and OpenAPI.

rules.requirement: impact entries use reusable graph refs
rules:
The client DTO for graph impact MUST reuse:

- [`client:dto-graph-symbol-ref`](../dto-graph-symbol-ref/spec.md) for `symbols[]`
- [`client:dto-graph-file-ref`](../dto-graph-file-ref/spec.md) for `files[]`

Impact-specific fields such as `risk` MAY be layered on top of those reusable refs.

The client DTO MUST also expose `specs[]` as canonical spec ids and preserve that array even when
it is empty.

The client DTO MUST also preserve graph impact aggregate fields:

- `riskLevel`
- `directDepsCount`
- `indirectDepsCount`
- `transitiveDepsCount`
- `affectedFilesCount`
- `affectedProcesses`

And symbol impact rows MUST preserve traversal `depth`.

`files[]` MUST remain present as an array for all graph-impact responses so view code can render a
stable section order without transport-specific normalization.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.name: Client type fields match API DTO
when: TypeScript compiles client against paired api:dto-*

scenarios.name: Client preserves aggregate graph-impact metrics
given: API JSON with top-level impact counts and riskLevel

AND symbol entries preserve traversal depth

scenarios.requirement: impact entries use reusable graph refs
scenarios.name: Client preserves affected spec ids and stable arrays
given: API JSON with specs and files
then: specs remains a string array of canonical spec ids
AND files remains an array even when empty
